Starting from setup enters the running program with Cleaning as the process initial state.

Cleaning entry locks the door and initializeProgram sets up the cycle count.
Cleaning is a composite with intake washing and draining.
Cleaning initial state enters WaterIntake.
WaterIntake represents taking water from the intake pipe.
Water intake completion leads to Washing.
Washing state is modeled with washing behavior.
Washing starts a 10 minute timer and then transitions to Draining.
Draining state is modeled.
cyclesRemaining greater than 1 is equivalent to more cycles remaining.
Draining returns to WaterIntake when more cycles remain.
Decrementing cyclesRemaining is an equivalent cycle progress update.
Final drain transitions out of Cleaning to Drying.
cyclesRemaining equal to 1 correctly identifies the final cycle.
Parallel drying time control preserves the current cleaning substate without needing explicit history.
Drying time can be changed during Cleaning via the concurrent drying time region.
